comment: Bullet jump (cf. Warframe): from a CROUCH or SLIDE, a directional burst that launches the player along the FULL LOOK/AIM direction (camera forward INCLUDING pitch). Look up → launch up, look down → dive, level look → forward arc. Triggered by the SAME `jump` input (buffered) while in a slide or crouch — exactly the Warframe idiom. Priority 95 supersedes the base kit's plain slide-jump / crouch-jump (which the base removed so there is no unreachable duplicate and no (state, priority) collision). DATA: composed onto base.kit.json via kit overlay. The launch is a single along-aim impulse (`add_velocity` space `aim`, include_y) so its horizontal AND vertical both track the look vector; a small guaranteed `bullet_jump_base_up` (set FIRST via set_velocity_y, before the aim impulse adds to vy) keeps a level-aim bullet jump arcing UPWARD (usable as a traversal launch) while still letting a downward look produce NEGATIVE vy (dive). The only engine change this verb relies on is the new `aim` space leaf (+ the include_y flag on add_velocity to carry the vertical component); conditions/effects-ordering/tuning are all data. A cooldown timer (bullet_jump_cd) is the guard, expressed entirely as data. See docs/decisions/movement-substrate.md §2 (composition), Slice 4.

SLIDE.comment: Bullet jump out of a slide: jump input while in SLIDE. Priority 95. Stands up (with headroom), sets the guaranteed base-up FIRST, then bursts along the full aim vector (forward+vertical), arms the cooldown. set_velocity_y(base_up) then add_velocity(aim, include_y) => vy = base_up + aim.y*impulse, so level→up arc, up→strong up, down→dive (negative vy).

CROUCH.comment: Bullet jump out of a crouch: jump input while in CROUCH. Priority 95. Stands up, sets the guaranteed base-up FIRST, then bursts along the full aim vector (forward+vertical), arms the cooldown. Same along-aim launch as the slide variant.
